new_classes.definition_en: Other undenatured spirituous beverages of an alcoholic strength by volume of less than 80% vol, not elsewhere classified under heading 2208, including aniseed spirits, raki, agave spirits other than tequila (e.g. mezcal), spirits distilled from aromatic herbs, digestive bitters, aquavit, kranawitter, spirits distilled from roots (e.g. gentian spirits), sorghum spirits, Hefebrand or lees spirit.

new_classes.label_en: Aniseed-based origin quality (spirit flavoured predominantly with aniseed)
new_classes.definition_en: Quality of a spirit whose predominant flavouring derives from aniseed, including aniseed spirits and raki.

new_classes.label_en: Agave origin quality (spirit distilled from agave plants, other than tequila)
new_classes.definition_en: Quality of a spirit derived from the distillation of fermented agave plant material, explicitly excluding tequila; covers mezcal and similar agave-based spirits.

new_classes.label_en: Aromatic herb or root distillation process (production of spirits from aromatic herbs, roots or similar botanicals)
new_classes.label_de: Destillationsprozess aus aromatischen Kräutern oder Wurzeln
new_classes.definition_en: Distillation process in which the base material consists of aromatic herbs, roots (e.g. gentian) or similar botanicals, yielding spirits such as Kräuterschnaps, aquavit, kranawitter or gentian spirit.

new_classes.label_en: Sorghum origin quality (spirit distilled from fermented sorghum)
new_classes.definition_en: Quality of a spirit whose base material is fermented sorghum grain.

new_classes.label_en: Hefebrand or lees origin quality (spirit distilled from yeast lees or fermentation lees)
new_classes.definition_en: Quality of a spirit derived from the distillation of yeast lees (Hefebrand) or other fermentation lees/trub.

new_classes.label_en: Other spirit distillation process (subheading 2208 90 56: aniseed, raki, agave excl. tequila, herbal, root, sorghum, lees)
new_classes.definition_en: Distillation process yielding spirits not classified elsewhere under heading 2208, including processes using aniseed, agave (non-tequila), aromatic herbs, roots, sorghum, or fermentation lees as the primary base material.

coverage_explanation:
The axioms cover the principal distinguishing criteria for subheading 2208 90 56: (1) membership in heading 2208 spirits (parent class SpiritsBeverages2208); (2) the "other" residual character expressed via complement restrictions excluding all named distillation processes of sibling subheadings (grape/marc, sugar-cane/rum, whisky types, gin, vodka, plum/pear/cherry, other-fruit, liqueur, arrack); (3) the undenatured and <80% vol alcoholic strength constraint; (4) exemplary quality markers for the main commodity types listed in the legal note (aniseed/raki, agave-non-tequila, sorghum, Hefebrand/lees); (5) a new distillation process class for aromatic herbs and roots (aquavit, kranawitter, gentian, etc.).

Gaps reducing the score: (a) The "complement" restriction pattern is applied on the process class level, but OWL-DL complement restrictions require careful use; they are expressed here as disjointness signals but cannot be used directly on a class-level someValuesFrom complement without a named class—these are captured as reminder axioms only. (b) Container-size sub-splits (≤2 L vs. >2 L) that appear in sibling codes 22089058/22089078 are not yet modelled here since the provided legal text only covers code 22089056 as a block. (c) The "digestive bitters" category is partially covered under AniseedBasedOriginQuality but deserves its own quality class; however, it overlaps with the existing SweetenedOrFlavouredQuality and is therefore left to that reusable class rather than being duplicated.
